text: When studying European geography, students often learn that the capital of France is Paris, a city famous for the Eiffel Tower and the Louvre museum.
Japan is an island nation in East Asia, and its capital city Tokyo is one of the most populous metropolitan areas in the world.
The capital of the United Kingdom is London, which sits on the River Thames and has been an important city for over two thousand years.
Germany is located in central Europe, and its capital Berlin became reunified after the fall of the Berlin Wall in nineteen eighty-nine.
Egypt's capital city is Cairo, located on the Nile River, and it is home to ancient monuments including the Great Pyramid at Giza.
When you add the numbers two and two together, the result is four, a basic arithmetic fact taught to children in elementary school.
The square root of sixteen is four, because four multiplied by four equals sixteen, demonstrating the inverse relationship between squaring and roots.
Multiplication is repeated addition, so ten times ten equals one hundred, just as adding ten to itself ten times gives the same result.
When dividing one hundred by four, you get twenty-five, which can be verified because four groups of twenty-five sum to one hundred.
In basic geometry, the angles of a triangle always sum to one hundred eighty degrees, regardless of the triangle's shape or size.
Pure water at standard atmospheric pressure boils at one hundred degrees Celsius, which corresponds to two hundred twelve degrees Fahrenheit.
Plants produce oxygen through a process called photosynthesis, in which they convert sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide into glucose and oxygen.
The chemical symbol for gold is Au, derived from the Latin word aurum, and it is one of the least reactive of the metallic elements.
Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system, with a mass more than two and a half times that of all the other planets combined.
DNA, short for deoxyribonucleic acid, is the molecule that carries genetic information in nearly all living organisms on Earth.
Water freezes into ice at zero degrees Celsius under standard atmospheric pressure, the same temperature at which ice melts back into water.
The human body has approximately two hundred and six bones in adulthood, although infants are born with around three hundred bones that fuse over time.
Light travels at approximately three hundred thousand kilometers per second in a vacuum, a speed that is the universal physical limit for information.
The continents of the Earth move slowly due to plate tectonics, a process that explains earthquakes, volcanoes, and the formation of mountain ranges.
Carbon dioxide is a gas composed of one carbon atom and two oxygen atoms, and it is produced when organisms breathe and when fuels are burned.
Shakespeare wrote a tragic play about two young lovers from feuding families called Romeo and Juliet, set in the Italian city of Verona.
The longest river in the world is generally considered to be the Nile, which flows northward through northeastern Africa for over six thousand kilometers.
The currency of the United Kingdom is the pound sterling, often abbreviated as GBP and represented by the symbol that resembles an ornate letter L.
The Great Wall of China stretches across northern China and was built over many centuries to protect against invasions from nomadic groups to the north.
George Washington was the first president of the United States, having previously commanded the Continental Army during the American Revolutionary War.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest of the world's oceans, covering approximately one third of the planet's surface area between Asia and the Americas.
Mount Everest is the highest mountain above sea level on Earth, located in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and the Tibet region of China.
The Roman Empire reached its greatest territorial extent under the emperor Trajan in the early second century, spanning from Britain to Mesopotamia.
Albert Einstein developed the theory of general relativity in nineteen fifteen, fundamentally changing our understanding of gravity, space, and time.
The Industrial Revolution began in Britain during the late eighteenth century and transformed economies through mechanization and the use of steam power.
Spanish is the official language of Spain and most countries in Latin America, making it one of the most widely spoken languages in the world.
The human heart has four chambers, two atria and two ventricles, which work together to pump blood through the body's circulatory system.
The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world, covering most of North Africa and spanning across multiple countries including Egypt, Libya, and Algeria.
Mozart was an Austrian composer of the Classical era who wrote over six hundred works, including symphonies, operas, and chamber music.
The atom consists of a nucleus containing protons and neutrons, surrounded by electrons that orbit at specific energy levels around the nucleus.
The Renaissance was a cultural movement that began in Italy in the fourteenth century and spread across Europe, marking a renewed interest in classical learning.
Penicillin, the first widely used antibiotic, was discovered by Alexander Fleming in nineteen twenty-eight when he noticed mold killing bacteria in a petri dish.
The speed of sound in dry air at twenty degrees Celsius is approximately three hundred forty-three meters per second, slower than the speed of light.
The Amazon rainforest is the largest tropical rainforest on Earth, located primarily in Brazil but extending into several other South American countries.
Isaac Newton formulated the three laws of motion and the law of universal gravitation, providing a mathematical framework that dominated physics for centuries.
The English alphabet contains twenty-six letters, divided into five vowels and twenty-one consonants, used to write the modern English language.
Australia is both a country and a continent, the largest country in Oceania and the only one to occupy an entire continent on its own.
The Eiffel Tower was completed in eighteen eighty-nine for the World's Fair held in Paris and was originally intended as a temporary structure.
Antarctica is the coldest continent on Earth, almost entirely covered by an ice sheet that contains about seventy percent of the world's fresh water.
The Mona Lisa is a portrait painted by Leonardo da Vinci in the early sixteenth century and is now displayed in the Louvre Museum in Paris.
The brain is the central organ of the human nervous system and contains roughly eighty-six billion neurons that communicate through electrical signals.
The Wright brothers achieved the first controlled, sustained powered flight in nineteen oh three at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina, with a flight lasting twelve seconds.
The Mediterranean Sea is bordered by southern Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia, and has been a crossroads of civilizations for thousands of years.
Photosynthesis takes place primarily in the chloroplasts of plant cells, where the green pigment chlorophyll absorbs light energy from the sun.
Mahatma Gandhi led the Indian independence movement against British rule in the early twentieth century using methods of nonviolent civil disobedience.
